title: 2024年度 第2回（準会場）英語資格検定2級 リーディング

vocabulary.example: Many of the older folk had jobs at the paper factory.
The children played a game where they pretended to be cats.
Will $50 be adequate for the cost of the gas?
The rainy weather ruined her plans for a picnic.
When his mother offered to pay him, he willingly agreed.
You might see something that inspires you.
David found it hard to move his limbs the next day.
I wonder how long this rainy weather is going to last.
The number of people suffering from depression is increasing.
I can't believe how much dust there is in this room.
Your shoes are falling apart, Stacey!
If we do that, we'll get in trouble with the boss.
Many English words are derived from foreign words.
As the screen became darker, the picture faded out.
I think there were about 30 in all.
I can't make a good pasta sauce, no matter what I try!
Be sure to watch out for cars.
Society had strict rules that told people how they should behave.
Strict rules told people how they should behave.
People thought it was not right to express feelings too much.
A special method of sharing feelings was called floriography.
Yellow flowers might show disappointment.

vocabulary.meaning: コミュニケーションする
vocabulary.example: People often communicate through texts and e-mails.
This effort is known as the Green Revolution.
It became difficult to feed everyone with traditional farming.
Plants need a chemical called nitrogen to grow.
Too much nitrogen polluted the environment.
Some people promote a movement to reduce environmental impact.
AI detects how much nitrogen each plant needs.
A one-month free membership fee for the Café Library Service.
Please show the coupon included with this e-mail.
Visit and explore our store to find books that interest you.
Blues is a type of music that started in the southern United States.
African Americans who were going through tough times.
They sang to express the difficulties they were having.
In Delta blues, singers play the guitar in a unique way.
The singer sings a line, and the audience respond to it.
Many moved to northern cities in search of job opportunities.
More people in crowded, noisy indoor environments could hear them.
Electric guitars became a symbol of Chicago blues.
Blues has also influenced different types of music.
Countries need a lot of space to put solar panels.
Solar panels do not cause air pollution.
Houses with solar panels can make electricity by themselves.
What advantages can this have?
Maybe you should follow a recipe next time.
Many doctors say people should exercise to help prevent it.
People are looking for more personal ways to express their feelings.
Scientists developed seeds that produced more crops.
A decrease in population led to fewer speakers.
Chemicals that helped plants grow were developed.

sections.name: 大問1
sections.instruction: 次の(1)から(17)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを1，2，3，4の中から一つ選びなさい。

questions.text: People under 40 years old in Evansville do not remember the paper factory because it closed before they were born. However, many of the older ( 1 ) had jobs there.
questions.translation: 40歳未満のエヴァンズビルの人々は製紙工場を覚えていない。閉鎖されたのは生まれる前だからだ。しかし年配の( 1 )の多くはそこで働いていた。
choiceAnalysis: folk=人々→正解。💡 older folk=年配の人々。folkは集合名詞
fame=名声→不適切
harm=害→不適切
humor=ユーモア→不適切

questions.text: The children played a game where they ( 2 ) to be cats. They had to find toy mice which were hidden in the garden.
questions.translation: 子供たちは猫の( 2 )をするゲームをした。庭に隠されたおもちゃのネズミを見つけなければならなかった。
choiceAnalysis: recovered=回復した→不適切
commented=コメントした→不適切
spoiled=台無しにした→不適切
pretended=ふりをした→正解。💡 pretend to be cats=猫のふりをする

questions.text:
A : Thank you for driving me to the airport today, Jim. Will $50 be ( 3 ) for the cost of the gas?
B : That's more than enough. Let me give you some change.
questions.translation:
A：今日空港まで送ってくれてありがとう、ジム。ガソリン代に50ドルで( 3 )かな？
B：多すぎるよ。おつりを渡すね。
choiceAnalysis: vague=漠然とした→不適切
adequate=十分な→正解。💡 more than enough=十分すぎる→adequateが正解
technical=技術的な→不適切
ridiculous=馬鹿げた→不適切

questions.text: Laura wanted to have a picnic in the park today, but the rainy weather ( 4 ) her plans. She had to stay home instead.
questions.translation: ローラは今日公園でピクニックをしたかったが、雨天が計画を( 4 )した。代わりに家にいなければならなかった。
choiceAnalysis: lowered=下げた→不適切
ruined=台無しにした→正解。💡 rainy weather+stay home instead=雨で計画が台無し
confirmed=確認した→不適切
traded=交換した→不適切

questions.text: Martin does not usually like doing jobs around the house, but when his mother offered to pay him to clean out the garage, he ( 5 ) agreed.
questions.translation: マーティンは普段家の仕事は好きではないが、母がガレージの掃除にお金を払うと言うと( 5 )同意した。
choiceAnalysis: willingly=喜んで→正解。💡 offered to pay+agreed=報酬があるので喜んで同意
cleverly=巧みに→不適切
currently=現在→不適切
relatively=比較的→不適切

questions.text:
A : Dad, I have to paint a picture for my art class, but I don't have any good ideas.
B : Why don't you go for a walk? You might see something that ( 6 ) you.
questions.translation:
A：パパ、美術の授業で絵を描かないといけないんだけど、いいアイデアがないの。
B：散歩に行ってみたら？何か( 6 )するものが見つかるかも。
choiceAnalysis: exceeds=超える→不適切
inspires=インスピレーションを与える→正解。💡 paint a picture+no ideas+walk=散歩で刺激を受ける
conceals=隠す→不適切
observes=観察する→不適切

questions.text: After going skiing for the first time in many years, David found it hard to move his ( 7 ) the next day. However, his arms and legs started to feel better later in the week.
questions.translation: 何年かぶりにスキーに行った後、翌日デイビッドは( 7 )を動かすのが難しかった。しかし週の後半には腕と脚が良くなり始めた。
choiceAnalysis: limbs=手足→正解。💡 arms and legs=腕と脚=limbs(手足)の言い換え
arches=アーチ→不適切
beliefs=信念→不適切
reforms=改革→不適切

questions.text:
A : I wonder how long this rainy weather is going to ( 8 ).
B : For a few more days, according to the weather report.
questions.translation:
A：この雨の天気はどれくらい( 8 )のかな。
B：天気予報によると、あと数日だよ。
choiceAnalysis: draw=描く→不適切
satisfy=満足させる→不適切
follow=従う→不適切
last=続く→正解。💡 how long+weather=天気がどれくらい続くか

questions.text: The number of people suffering from ( 9 ) is increasing. Many doctors say that people should try to get more exercise to help prevent it.
questions.translation: ( 9 )に苦しむ人の数が増加している。多くの医師が予防のためにもっと運動すべきだと言っている。
choiceAnalysis: suspicion=疑い→不適切
connection=つながり→不適切
success=成功→不適切
depression=うつ病→正解。💡 suffering+exercise to prevent=うつ病

questions.text:
A : I can't believe how much ( 10 ) there is in this room. I just cleaned it yesterday.
B : I know. It's hard to keep this place clean.
questions.translation:
A：この部屋にこんなに( 10 )があるなんて信じられない。昨日掃除したばかりなのに。
B：わかるよ。きれいに保つのは難しいんだ。
choiceAnalysis: faith=信仰→不適切
dust=ほこり→正解。💡 cleaned yesterday+hard to keep clean=ほこり
budget=予算→不適切
debt=借金→不適切

questions.text:
A : Your shoes are ( 11 ), Stacey! You should get some new ones.
B : Yeah, I know they're getting old, but I still really like them.
questions.translation:
A：靴が( 11 )よ、ステイシー！新しいのを買った方がいいわ。
B：うん、古くなってるのはわかるけど、まだ気に入ってるの。
choiceAnalysis: falling apart=ばらばらになっている→正解。💡 shoes+getting old+get new ones=靴がボロボロ
picking up=拾う→不適切
trying out=試す→不適切
settling down=落ち着く→不適切

questions.text:
A : Hey, Bill, let's finish the report tomorrow.
B : If we do that, we'll ( 12 ) the boss. He told us to send it to him today.
questions.translation:
A：ビル、レポートは明日仕上げよう。
B：そうしたら上司と( 12 )よ。今日中に送れって言われたんだ。
choiceAnalysis: get through to=通じる→不適切
get in trouble with=～とトラブルになる→正解。💡 boss told us today+finish tomorrow=上司とトラブル
be cut out for=向いている→不適切
be of service to=役に立つ→不適切

questions.text: Many English words are ( 13 ) foreign words. For example, "television" was made by combining a Greek word meaning "far" and a Latin word meaning "sight."
questions.translation: 多くの英単語は外国語( 13 )。例えば「television」はギリシャ語の「遠い」とラテン語の「視覚」を組み合わせて作られた。
choiceAnalysis: parted with=手放した→不適切
faced by=直面した→不適切
derived from=～に由来する→正解。💡 combining Greek+Latin=外国語に由来する
opposed to=反対した→不適切

questions.text: The movie ended by showing a beautiful sunset. As the screen became darker, the picture ( 14 ).
questions.translation: 映画は美しい夕日を映して終わった。画面が暗くなると、映像は( 14 )。
choiceAnalysis: held on=つかまった→不適切
gave away=譲った→不適切
faded out=消えていった→正解。💡 sunset+screen darker=映像がフェードアウト
took over=引き継いだ→不適切

questions.text:
A : How many people came to your party last night?
B : I'm not sure, but I think there were about 30 ( 15 ). I didn't count them.
questions.translation:
A：昨夜のパーティーには何人来たの？
B：よくわからないけど、( 15 )30人くらいだったと思う。数えなかったんだ。
choiceAnalysis: for instance=例えば→不適切
on top=その上→不適切
in all=全部で→正解。💡 about 30+didn't count=全部で約30人
by chance=偶然に→不適切

questions.text:
A : I can't make a good pasta sauce, ( 16 ) what I try!
B : Maybe you should follow a recipe next time.
questions.translation:
A：何を試しても良いパスタソースが作れないの、( 16 )！
B：次はレシピに従った方がいいかもね。
choiceAnalysis: no better=より良くない→不適切
no matter=～に関わらず→正解。💡 no matter what I try=何を試しても
deciding on=決める→不適切
based on=～に基づく→不適切

questions.text:
A : Are you taking your bicycle to school, Ashley? Be sure to ( 17 ) for cars.
B : OK, Dad. I'll be careful.
questions.translation:
A：自転車で学校に行くの、アシュリー？車に( 17 )ね。
B：うん、パパ。気をつけるね。
choiceAnalysis: wear out=すり減る→不適切
get over=乗り越える→不適切
watch out=気をつける→正解。💡 bicycle+be careful+cars=車に気をつける
go out=出かける→不適切

sections.name: 大問2
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，Bを読み，その文意にそって(18)から(23)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: The Secret Language of Flowers
paragraphs: In the nineteenth century in the United Kingdom, society had strict rules that told people how they should behave. These included rules about how to dress, how to eat, and how to speak properly. One of the most important rules was about ( 18 ). People thought it was not right to express feelings too much, especially in public. Because of this, people had to find other ways to share how they felt in order to respect society's rules.

During this time, a special method of sharing feelings was used. This method was called "floriography," which means the language of flowers. Each flower had its own meaning, which let people send secret messages that could share love, friendship, or even a hidden dislike without saying anything. For example, giving someone red flowers could mean deep love, while giving yellow flowers might show disappointment or not being happy with someone. This became a useful way for British people at that time to share their feelings ( 19 ).

Today, people often communicate through texts and e-mails, but the full meaning of people's thoughts is not always clear with just written words. ( 20 ), floriography is becoming popular again. People are starting to see the value of using flowers to communicate. This is because each flower can hold a deep message that might be difficult to express with only words. It is likely that the new interest in floriography shows that people are looking for more personal ways to express their feelings to others.
translations: 19世紀の英国では、人々の振る舞いを規定する厳しい規則が社会にあった。服装、食事、話し方に関する規則が含まれていた。最も重要な規則の一つは( 18 )についてだった。人々は感情を表に出しすぎるのは正しくないと考えた。そのため社会のルールを尊重しつつ感情を共有する方法を見つけなければならなかった。
この時代、感情を共有する特別な方法が使われた。「フロリオグラフィー」と呼ばれる花言葉だ。各花には意味があり、愛、友情、さらには隠れた嫌悪感さえも言葉なしに伝えられた。例えば赤い花は深い愛を、黄色い花は失望を示すことがあった。これは当時の英国人が( 19 )感情を共有する有用な方法となった。
今日、人々はテキストやメールで通信するが、書かれた言葉だけでは考えの全容が常に明確とは限らない。( 20 )、フロリオグラフィーが再び人気になっている。花を使ったコミュニケーションの価値に人々が気づき始めている。各花が言葉だけでは表現しにくい深いメッセージを持つことができるからだ。
sentencePairs: Society had strict rules that told people how they should behave.
人々の振る舞いを規定する厳しいルールが社会にあった。
People thought it was not right to express feelings too much.
感情を表に出しすぎるのは正しくないと考えた。
Each flower had its own meaning.
各花には独自の意味があった。
Red flowers could mean deep love.
赤い花は深い愛を意味した。
Floriography is becoming popular again.
フロリオグラフィーが再び人気になっている。
People are looking for more personal ways to express their feelings.
人々はより個人的な感情表現方法を探している。

choiceAnalysis: 他者に礼儀正しく→不適切
年配者に敬意を示す→不適切
嘘をつかない→不適切
感情を公に見せない→正解。💡 not right to express feelings too much=感情を表に出さない

choices: with only their friends and family
toward people they did not know
choiceAnalysis: 友人や家族だけと→不適切
社会規範に反せず→正解。💡 in order to respect society's rules=社会ルールを守りつつ
言葉にして→花で伝えるので逆
知らない人に向けて→不適切
このため→正解。💡 written words not always clear→Due to this→flowers popular=文字では不十分→そのため花言葉が復活
反対に→不適切
同時に→不適切
結論として→途中なので不適切

passages.label: B
passages.title: Green Revolution
paragraphs: After World War II, the world's population grew quickly. ( 21 ), it became more and more difficult to feed everyone with traditional farming. In countries like India and the Philippines, where population growth was a big challenge, they looked for better ways to produce food. This effort is known as the Green Revolution. During this revolution, scientists developed seeds that produced more crops, chemicals that helped plants grow, and chemicals that got rid of insects. These quickly began to be used all over the world.

Plants need a chemical called nitrogen to grow. They use their roots to take in nitrogen that is found in the soil. Chemicals developed during the Green Revolution used a lot of nitrogen. ( 22 ), those special chemicals helped plants grow, but sometimes, people used too much at the wrong time. This made nitrogen go into the land, rivers, and seas, which polluted the environment.

Nowadays, to reduce the impact on the environment, some people promote a movement that recommends that people follow four "rights." It encourages people to use the right chemicals in the right amount, at the right time, and in the right place. In some areas, AI has been introduced to help people do this. Instead of spreading equal amounts of nitrogen across the land where crops are grown, AI detects how much nitrogen each plant needs at all times. This helps prevent farmers from adding more nitrogen ( 23 ).
translations: 第二次世界大戦後、世界の人口は急速に増加した。( 21 )、伝統的な農業だけでは全員を養うのがますます困難になった。人口増加が大きな課題だったインドやフィリピンのような国では、食料生産のより良い方法を模索した。この取り組みは「緑の革命」として知られている。科学者たちはより多くの作物を生産する種、植物の成長を助ける化学物質、害虫を駆除する化学物質を開発した。
植物は成長に窒素という化学物質を必要とする。根を使って土壌中の窒素を吸収する。緑の革命で開発された化学物質は多くの窒素を使用した。( 22 )、それらの特別な化学物質は植物の成長を助けたが、時に人々は間違った時期に使いすぎた。これにより窒素が土地、川、海に流れ込み環境を汚染した。
今日、環境への影響を減らすため、4つの「正しいこと」を推奨する運動を推進する人がいる。適切な化学物質を適切な量、適切な時期、適切な場所で使うことを奨励する。一部の地域ではAIが導入されている。窒素を均等にまく代わりに、AIが各植物がいつどれだけの窒素を必要とするかを検出する。これにより農家が( 23 )窒素を追加しすぎることを防ぐ。
sentencePairs: After World War II, the world's population grew quickly.
第二次世界大戦後、世界の人口は急速に増加した。
This effort is known as the Green Revolution.
この取り組みは緑の革命として知られている。
Plants need a chemical called nitrogen to grow.
植物は成長に窒素という化学物質を必要とする。
Sometimes people used too much at the wrong time.
時に人々は間違った時期に使いすぎた。
AI detects how much nitrogen each plant needs at all times.
AIが各植物がいつどれだけの窒素を必要とするかを検出する。

choiceAnalysis: 農家が多かったので→不適切
十分な食料があったが→逆
より多くの人が食料を必要としたため→正解。💡 population grew→more people needed food→difficult to feed
人々が自由になった今→不適切
残念ながら→不適切
したがって→正解。💡 used a lot of nitrogen→Therefore helped plants grow(窒素使用→結果成長)
驚くべきことに→不適切
それにもかかわらず→不適切

choices: if adding it is too expensive
when the crops are still too young
that has not been tested properly
choiceAnalysis: すでに十分なところに→正解。💡 detects how much each plant needs=必要量検出→すでに十分な場所への過剰投入を防ぐ
費用が高すぎる場合→不適切
作物がまだ若すぎるとき→不適切
適切にテストされていない→不適切

sections.name: 大問3
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，Bの内容に関して，(24)から(31)までの質問に対して最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: Introducing Café Library Service
paragraphs: Greetings to Bless Bookstore Fan Club members! We have asked you several times to tell us what new services you would like us to offer through the store. Finally, we're excited to tell you about a new service that many people have asked for. Our goal is to make you happy and make your time at the bookstore better. Thanks for telling us what you think.

The name of the new service is Café Library Service. With this service, you can read as many books from our store as you like at the new café in the building for a fee of $10 a month. This café will open at the beginning of next month on the first floor of the store. Anyone can use the café, but only Bless Bookstore Fan Club members can take books from the store and read them in the café. Members can bring up to three books into the café at a time.

Since you've been customers at our bookstore for a long time, we want to give you a special gift to say thank you. Please show the coupon included with this e-mail to a staff member to receive a one-month free membership fee for the Café Library Service and a free drink at the café. The special deal will end at the end of next month. Visit and explore our store to find books that interest you.
Best regards,
Bless Bookstore Customer Service
translations: ブレス書店ファンクラブ会員の皆様！これまで何度か、店舗を通じてどのような新サービスを望むかお聞きしてきました。ついに多くの方がリクエストした新サービスをお知らせできることを嬉しく思います。皆様を幸せにし、書店での時間をより良くすることが私たちの目標です。ご意見をお聞かせいただきありがとうございます。
新サービスの名前は「カフェ図書サービス」です。このサービスでは月額10ドルで、ビル内の新しいカフェで当店の本を好きなだけ読むことができます。カフェは来月初めに店舗の1階にオープンします。カフェは誰でも利用できますが、店の本を持ち込んで読めるのはブレス書店ファンクラブ会員のみです。会員は一度に最大3冊までカフェに持ち込めます。
長年のお客様への感謝の気持ちとして、特別なギフトをお贈りします。このメールに添付のクーポンをスタッフに見せていただくと、カフェ図書サービスの1ヶ月無料会費とカフェでの無料ドリンクを受け取れます。この特別オファーは来月末で終了します。
sentencePairs: We have asked you several times what new services you would like.
これまで何度かどのような新サービスを望むかお聞きしました。
Many people have asked for this new service.
多くの方がこの新サービスをリクエストしました。
You can read as many books as you like at the new café.
新しいカフェで好きなだけ本を読めます。
Members can bring up to three books into the café at a time.
会員は一度に最大3冊まで持ち込めます。
Show the coupon to receive a free membership and a free drink.
クーポンを見せると無料会費と無料ドリンクがもらえます。

questions.question: What is one reason the store is starting a new service?
choices: To compete with other bookstores in the area.
To answer the wishes of the customers.
To talk about trends in bookstores around the country.
To celebrate the store's important anniversary.
choiceAnalysis: 他店との競争→本文にない
顧客の要望に応える→正解。💡 many people have asked for=多くの人がリクエストした
全国の書店トレンド→本文にない
記念日のお祝い→本文にない

questions.question: Bless Bookstore Fan Club members can
choices: bring no more than three books into the café at once.
leave books that they have not finished in the café.
use the café earlier than other customers.
get a discount when buying special books.
choiceAnalysis: 一度に3冊まで持ち込み可→正解。💡 bring up to three books at a time
読みかけの本を置いていける→本文にない
他の客より早く利用→本文にない
特別割引→本文にない

questions.question: How can fan club members get a free drink?
choices: By purchasing a drink from a selected list.
By visiting the café on the opening day.
By presenting a coupon included in the e-mail.
By signing up for a one-month trial membership.
choiceAnalysis: 選んだリストから購入→不適切
開店日に訪問→不適切
メール添付のクーポンを提示→正解。💡 show the coupon included with this e-mail=メールのクーポンを見せる
1ヶ月体験入会→不適切

passages.label: B
passages.title: Blues
paragraphs: Blues is a type of music that started in the southern part of the United States over one hundred years ago. It was made by African Americans who were going through tough times. They sang to share their sad feelings and express the difficulties they were having. However, blues is not only about unhappy feelings. Instead, it is also about not giving up in difficult times. Over time, the music has spread to different parts of the country, resulting in various types of blues music.

Delta blues is the original type of blues, and it is still considered the main type of blues. In Delta blues, singers play the guitar in a unique way. They do this by using a glass tool on their fingers. By sliding it along the guitar strings, they make special sounds. The original Delta blues singers also came up with a special singing style called "call and response." This is a form where the singer sings a line, and other band members and the audience respond to it.

In the twentieth century, many African Americans on southern farms moved to northern cities, including Chicago, in search of job opportunities and a better life. They brought blues music with them but hoped to create something new. Instead of singing about the difficulties of life on farms, they began to sing about everyday city life. They started to use electric guitars so that more people in crowded, noisy indoor environments could hear them. The use of electric guitars became a symbol of Chicago blues.

Blues has not only developed into different types of blues itself but also influenced different types of music. Delta and Chicago blues both focus on guitar music, and this influence can be seen in modern rock music. Also, the strong expression of feelings has been passed down to rock music. Expressing emotions is a big part of rap music, too. Just like in blues, rap artists openly talk about their experiences and feelings, often sharing social messages, which was a big part of the original Delta blues.
translations: ブルースは100年以上前にアメリカ南部で生まれた音楽だ。困難な時代を過ごしていたアフリカ系アメリカ人によって作られた。彼らは悲しい気持ちを分かち合い困難を表現するために歌った。しかしブルースは不幸な感情だけではない。困難な時に諦めないことについてでもある。音楽は国内各地に広がり、さまざまな種類のブルースが生まれた。
デルタ・ブルースはブルースの原型で、今でも主要な種類と見なされている。デルタ・ブルースでは独特のギター奏法を用いる。指にガラスの道具を使い、弦の上を滑らせて特別な音を出す。オリジナルのデルタ・ブルース歌手は「コール・アンド・レスポンス」と呼ばれる歌唱スタイルも生み出した。歌手が1フレーズ歌い、バンドメンバーと聴衆がそれに応答する形式だ。
20世紀、南部の農場にいた多くのアフリカ系アメリカ人がシカゴなどの北部都市に移住した。仕事と良い生活を求めてだ。ブルース音楽を持っていったが新しいものを創りたいと思った。農場生活の困難ではなく日常の都市生活を歌い始めた。混雑した騒がしい室内環境でもっと多くの人に聞こえるようエレキギターを使い始めた。エレキギターの使用はシカゴ・ブルースの象徴となった。
ブルースはブルース自体のさまざまな種類に発展しただけでなく、他の音楽にも影響を与えた。デルタとシカゴのブルースはどちらもギター音楽に焦点を当てており、その影響は現代のロック音楽に見られる。また感情の強い表現はロック音楽に受け継がれた。感情の表現はラップ音楽の大きな部分でもある。ブルースと同様、ラップアーティストは経験や感情について率直に語り、社会的メッセージを共有する。
sentencePairs: Blues started in the southern part of the United States over one hundred years ago.
ブルースは100年以上前にアメリカ南部で生まれた。
Blues is not only about unhappy feelings but also about not giving up.
ブルースは不幸な感情だけでなく諦めないことについてでもある。
In Delta blues, singers play the guitar in a unique way.
デルタ・ブルースでは独特のギター奏法を用いる。
They started to use electric guitars in crowded environments.
混雑した環境でエレキギターを使い始めた。
Electric guitars became a symbol of Chicago blues.
エレキギターはシカゴ・ブルースの象徴となった。
Blues has also influenced different types of music.
ブルースは他の音楽にも影響を与えた。
Rap artists openly talk about their experiences and feelings.
ラップアーティストは経験や感情を率直に語る。

questions.question: Why did African Americans create the music form called blues?
choices: To help people of all races make the economy better.
To teach other people about the hometowns they came from.
To earn money in the music industry that was starting to develop.
To celebrate the effort they made to not give up when things were difficult.
choiceAnalysis: 全人種の経済支援→不適切
故郷を教える→不適切
音楽産業で稼ぐ→不適切
困難な時に諦めない努力を称える→正解。💡 not only unhappy feelings but also about not giving up=諦めない精神の表現

questions.question: In Delta blues,
choices: singers encourage the audience to participate in the music together.
audience members bring glass tools and use them as musical instruments.
each band member has to play a number of different musical instruments.
the use of guitars only became popular years after they were invented.
choiceAnalysis: 聴衆も音楽に参加→正解。💡 call and response=歌手が歌い聴衆が応答=参加を促す
聴衆がガラス道具→歌手が使う
各メンバーが複数楽器→不適切
ギター人気は後→不適切

questions.question: What was one reason why Chicago blues musicians used electric guitars?
choices: Electric guitars were becoming cheaper in the areas where they lived.
Some businesses gave guitars to these new musicians to support them.
The sound of electric guitars was more suitable for performance on TV.
They played in places where it was difficult for people to hear them.
choiceAnalysis: エレキギターが安い→不適切
企業がギターを提供→不適切
TV演奏に適した音→不適切
聞こえにくい場所で演奏→正解。💡 crowded noisy indoor environments=混雑した騒がしい室内で聞こえるように

questions.question: In what way is blues similar to rap music?
choices: They were both started by African Americans in the southern United States.
There are usually more than one guitarist and singer in a band.
Musicians express their feelings and share a message through their songs.
The songs reflect the difficult lives that women usually face.
choiceAnalysis: 両方アフリカ系が南部で始めた→ラップについては不明
ギタリストと歌手が複数→不適切
音楽家が感情を表現しメッセージを共有→正解。💡 openly talk about experiences and feelings, sharing social messages=感情・社会的メッセージの共有
女性の困難な生活→不適切

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true?
choices: Delta blues songs are usually about life in big cities.
Blues music had much more influence on rap music than on rock.
The songs in blues music avoid talking about difficult times or subjects.
Blues music developed as the people moved from one place to another.
choiceAnalysis: デルタ・ブルースは都市生活→農場生活なので逆
ロックよりラップへの影響→両方に影響
困難なテーマを避ける→逆
人々が移動するにつれて発展→正解。💡 south→north cities=南部から北部への移動とともに発展

focusPoints.title: 形式主語 It + be + C + to V
focusPoints.subtitle: Introductory It with Infinitive
focusPoints.explanation: It is/was/became + 形容詞 + to V は「Vすることは〜だ」。真の主語はto以下で、形式主語itが文頭に置かれる。長文で状況の評価・判断を述べる際に頻出。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: As more people needed food, it became more and more difficult to feed everyone with traditional farming.

examples.en: As more people needed food, it became more and more difficult to feed everyone with traditional farming.
examples.ja: より多くの人が食料を必要としたため、従来の農業では全員を養うことがますます困難になった。
examples.note: it became + more and more difficult + to feed: 形式主語+比較級の反復で困難さの増大を表現

examples.en: It is likely that the new interest in floriography shows people are looking for more personal ways to express feelings.
examples.ja: 花言葉への新しい関心は、人々がより個人的な感情表現の方法を求めていることを示している可能性が高い。
examples.note: It is likely that: 形式主語+that節。「〜する可能性が高い」

examples.en: It is important to understand both sides of the argument.
examples.ja: 議論の両面を理解することが重要だ。
examples.note: It is + 形容詞 + to V: 最も基本的な形式主語構文

practiceQuestions.q: 'it became more and more difficult to feed everyone' の構文を分析してください。
practiceQuestions.a: it(形式主語) + became(V) + more and more difficult(C: 比較級反復) + to feed everyone(真の主語)。「全員を養うことがますます困難になった」。

practiceQuestions.q: 'more and more difficult' はどのような表現ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 比較級の反復。「ますます〜」と程度が徐々に増すことを表す。= increasingly difficult。

practiceQuestions.q: 緑の革命で科学者が開発したものを3つ答えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: ①より多くの作物を生産する種子(seeds that produced more crops) ②植物の成長を助ける化学物質 ③害虫を駆除する化学物質(chemicals that got rid of insects)

practiceQuestions.q: 'This effort is known as the Green Revolution' の 'is known as' はどのような表現ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 「〜として知られている」。受動態+as。This effort（この取り組み）が「緑の革命」と呼ばれていることを示す。

highlightPatterns: it became more and more difficult to feed everyone with traditional farming

practicePassage.en:
[出典: Green Revolution]
After World War II, the world's population grew quickly. As more people needed food, it became more and more difficult to feed everyone with traditional farming. In countries like India and the Philippines, where population growth was a big challenge, they looked for better ways to produce food. This effort is known as the Green Revolution. During this revolution, scientists developed seeds that produced more crops, chemicals that helped plants grow, and chemicals that got rid of insects. These quickly began to be used all over the world.
practicePassage.ja: 第二次世界大戦後、世界の人口は急速に増加した。より多くの人が食料を必要としたため、従来の農業では全員を養うことがますます困難になった。インドやフィリピンなど人口増加が大きな課題であった国々では、食料をより効率的に生産する方法を模索した。この取り組みは緑の革命として知られている。この革命の間に、科学者たちはより多くの作物を生産する種子、植物の成長を助ける化学物質、害虫を駆除する化学物質を開発した。これらはすぐに世界中で使われ始めた。

focusPoints.title: 結果の分詞構文（resulting in / -ing）
focusPoints.subtitle: Participial Clause of Result
focusPoints.explanation: resulting in / 文末の -ing は「その結果〜になる」と前文の帰結を述べる分詞構文。長文の因果関係の把握に重要。not only A but also B との組み合わせも頻出。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Over time, the music has spread to different parts of the country, resulting in various types of blues music.

examples.en: Over time, the music has spread to different parts of the country, resulting in various types of blues music.
examples.ja: 時が経つにつれ、この音楽は国の様々な地域に広がり、その結果、多様な種類のブルース音楽が生まれた。
examples.note: resulting in: 「その結果〜になった」結果の分詞構文

examples.en: Blues is not only about unhappy feelings. Instead, it is also about not giving up in difficult times.
examples.ja: ブルースは不幸な感情だけのものではない。むしろ、困難な時でも諦めないことでもある。
examples.note: not only A. Instead, it is also B: AだけでなくBでもある。Insteadで追加情報

examples.en: The heavy rain flooded the streets, causing major traffic delays.
examples.ja: 大雨が通りを浸水させ、その結果、大規模な交通渋滞が発生した。
examples.note: causing: 結果の分詞構文。= and it caused

practiceQuestions.q: 'resulting in various types of blues music' の 'resulting in' はどのような用法ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 結果の分詞構文。= and this resulted in。前文（音楽が広がった）の結果として「多様なブルースが生まれた」。

practiceQuestions.q: ブルースは「不幸な感情だけのもの」ではないと筆者は述べています。他に何を表していますか？
practiceQuestions.a: 困難な時でも諦めないこと（not giving up in difficult times）。ブルースには希望や粘り強さのメッセージもある。

practiceQuestions.q: 'They sang to share their sad feelings and express the difficulties' の 'to share' と 'express' はどのような用法ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 目的の不定詞。「悲しい気持ちを分かち合い、困難を表現するために」歌った。to share と(to) express が並列。

practiceQuestions.q: 'who were going through tough times' の 'go through' はどのような意味ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 「〜を経験する、乗り越える」。句動詞。困難な時期を体験していたアフリカ系アメリカ人を修飾する関係詞節。

highlightPatterns: resulting in various types of blues music

practicePassage.en:
[出典: Blues]
Blues is a type of music that started in the southern part of the United States over one hundred years ago. It was made by African Americans who were going through tough times. They sang to share their sad feelings and express the difficulties they were having. However, blues is not only about unhappy feelings. Instead, it is also about not giving up in difficult times. Over time, the music has spread to different parts of the country, resulting in various types of blues music.
practicePassage.ja: ブルースは100年以上前にアメリカ南部で生まれた音楽の一種である。困難な時期を乗り越えようとしていたアフリカ系アメリカ人によって作られた。彼らは悲しい気持ちを分かち合い、経験している困難を表現するために歌った。しかし、ブルースは不幸な感情だけのものではない。むしろ、困難な時でも諦めないことでもある。時が経つにつれ、この音楽は国の様々な地域に広がり、その結果、多様な種類のブルース音楽が生まれた。

focusPoints.title: 目的の so that + 手段の by -ing
focusPoints.subtitle: Purpose and Means Expressions
focusPoints.explanation: so that + S + could は「〜できるように」と目的を表す。by -ing は「〜することによって」と手段を表す。Instead of + -ing は「〜する代わりに」。行動の理由と方法を理解するのに重要。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: They started to use electric guitars so that more people in crowded, noisy indoor environments could hear them.

examples.en: They started to use electric guitars so that more people in crowded, noisy indoor environments could hear them.
examples.ja: 混雑した騒がしい室内環境でより多くの人が聞こえるように、彼らはエレキギターを使い始めた。
examples.note: so that + S + could: 「〜できるように」目的の副詞節

examples.en: Instead of singing about the difficulties of life on farms, they began to sing about everyday city life.
examples.ja: 農場生活の困難について歌う代わりに、日常の都市生活について歌い始めた。
examples.note: Instead of + -ing: 「〜する代わりに」代替・対比

examples.en: By sliding it along the guitar strings, they make special sounds.
examples.ja: それをギターの弦に沿って滑らせることで、特別な音を出す。
examples.note: By + -ing: 「〜することによって」手段の表現

practiceQuestions.q: 'so that more people ... could hear them' の 'so that' は何を表していますか？
practiceQuestions.a: 目的。「より多くの人が聞こえるように」エレキギターを使い始めた。so that + could で過去の目的を表す。

practiceQuestions.q: 'Instead of singing about the difficulties of life on farms' と対比されている内容は何ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 日常の都市生活について歌うこと（began to sing about everyday city life）。農場 → 都市という歌のテーマの変化。

practiceQuestions.q: アフリカ系アメリカ人が北部の都市に移住した理由を2つ答えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: ①仕事の機会を求めて(in search of job opportunities) ②より良い生活を求めて(a better life)。

practiceQuestions.q: エレキギターの使用がシカゴブルースにとって重要な理由は何ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 混雑した騒がしい室内環境で音が届くようにするため。エレキギターの使用はシカゴブルースの象徴(symbol)となった。

highlightPatterns: so that more people in crowded, noisy indoor environments could hear them
Instead of singing about the difficulties of life on farms

practicePassage.en:
[出典: Blues]
In the twentieth century, many African Americans on southern farms moved to northern cities, including Chicago, in search of job opportunities and a better life. They brought blues music with them but hoped to create something new. Instead of singing about the difficulties of life on farms, they began to sing about everyday city life. They started to use electric guitars so that more people in crowded, noisy indoor environments could hear them. The use of electric guitars became a symbol of Chicago blues.
practicePassage.ja: 20世紀に、南部の農場にいた多くのアフリカ系アメリカ人が、仕事の機会とより良い生活を求めてシカゴを含む北部の都市に移り住んだ。彼らはブルース音楽を持ち込んだが、何か新しいものを作りたいと望んでいた。農場生活の困難について歌う代わりに、日常の都市生活について歌い始めた。混雑した騒がしい室内環境でより多くの人が聞こえるように、エレキギターを使い始めた。エレキギターの使用はシカゴブルースの象徴となった。

focusPoints.title: 提案の that 節（recommend/suggest + that S + V原形）
focusPoints.subtitle: Subjunctive in Suggestion Clauses
focusPoints.explanation: recommend/suggest/propose + that + S + V原形は、提案・要求・推薦を表す仮定法現在。shouldが省略された形でフォーマルな文章に多い。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Some people promote a movement that recommends that people follow four 'rights.'

examples.en: Some people promote a movement that recommends that people follow four 'rights.'
examples.ja: 一部の人はは4つの「正しいこと」に従うことを推奨する運動を推進している。
examples.note: recommends that people follow: 提案のthat節。followは原形（shouldの省略）

examples.en: This helps prevent farmers from adding more nitrogen where there is already enough.
examples.ja: これにより、すでに十分な窒素がある場所に農家がさらに窒素を加えるのを防ぐのに役立つ。
examples.note: prevent + O + from + -ing: 「Oが〜するのを防ぐ」

examples.en: The doctor suggested that she take a week off work.
examples.ja: 医師は彼女に1週間仕事を休むよう提案した。
examples.note: suggested that she take(原形): 仮定法現在。takesではなくtake。

practiceQuestions.q: 'recommends that people follow four rights' の 'follow' はなぜ原形ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: 提案のthat節（仮定法現在）。recommend/suggest/propose + that + S + V原形。followsではなくfollow。should followのshouldが省略された形。

practiceQuestions.q: 4つの「正しいこと」(rights)を全て答えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: ①正しい化学物質(the right chemicals) ②正しい量(the right amount) ③正しい時期(the right time) ④正しい場所(the right place)

practiceQuestions.q: AIは農業でどのように役立っていますか？
practiceQuestions.a: 土地全体に均等に窒素を撒く代わりに、各植物が必要な窒素量を検出し、過剰な窒素の散布を防いでいる。

practiceQuestions.q: 'Instead of spreading equal amounts of nitrogen across the land' の 'Instead of' は何と対比していますか？
practiceQuestions.a: AI導入後の方法（各植物の窒素必要量を個別に検出すること）と対比。従来の均一散布→AIによる個別最適化。

practicePassage.en:
[出典: Green Revolution]
Nowadays, to reduce the impact on the environment, some people promote a movement that recommends that people follow four "rights." It encourages people to use the right chemicals in the right amount, at the right time, and in the right place. In some areas, AI has been introduced to help people do this. Instead of spreading equal amounts of nitrogen across the land where crops are grown, AI detects how much nitrogen each plant needs at all times. This helps prevent farmers from adding more nitrogen where there is already enough.
practicePassage.ja: 現在、環境への影響を減らすため、4つの「正しいこと」に従うことを推奨する運動を推進している人々がいる。正しい化学物質を、正しい量で、正しい時期に、正しい場所で使うことを奨励している。一部の地域では、これを実現するためにAIが導入されている。作物が育つ土地全体に均等な量の窒素を散布する代わりに、AIが各植物が常にどれだけの窒素を必要としているかを検出する。これにより、すでに十分な窒素がある場所に農家がさらに窒素を加えるのを防ぐのに役立つ。

focusPoints.title: 今回の重要なパラフレーズ
focusPoints.subtitle: Key Paraphrases
focusPoints.explanation: 英語資格検定2級では本文の表現が設問で別の言葉に言い換えられる。本文と選択肢の対応を見抜く力が合格の鍵。
focusPoints.sourceQuote:
①本文: a popular way to communicate feelings that could not be spoken aloud
→選択肢: express emotions that were socially unacceptable to say directly
②本文: Scientists developed new types of crops that could produce more food
→選択肢: new varieties were created to increase agricultural output
③本文: The library will lend books to customers while they enjoy their drinks
→選択肢: Patrons can borrow reading materials during their café visit
④本文: Blues singers expressed the hardships of their daily lives through music
→選択肢: Musicians used songs to communicate their difficult experiences

examples.ja: 感情を伝える → 感情を表現する

examples.en: Scientists developed new types of crops → new varieties were created
examples.ja: 科学者が新品種を開発した → 新品種が作られた
examples.note: developed = created（能動→受動のパラフレーズ）

examples.en: lend books to customers → Patrons can borrow reading materials
examples.ja: 客に本を貸す → 利用者が読み物を借りられる

practiceQuestions.q: 'has not only developed into ... but also influenced' を言い換えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: 'has both evolved into ... and impacted'。developed = evolved, influenced = impacted。not only...but also構文。

practiceQuestions.q: 'different types of blues' を言い換えてください。
practiceQuestions.a: 'various styles of blues music' や 'multiple forms of blues'。different types = various styles / multiple forms。

practiceQuestions.q: 'rock and roll, jazz, and soul music' はブルースとどのような関係ですか？
practiceQuestions.a: ブルースから影響を受けて生まれたジャンル。ブルースのリズム、コード進行、感情表現が基盤。

practiceQuestions.q: ブルースが音楽史に与えた影響を要約してください。
practiceQuestions.a: 複数のブルース形態に発展。ロックンロール、ジャズ、ソウルなど多くの現代音楽の基盤。世界的に認められた遺産。

practicePassage.en:
[出典: Blues]
Blues has not only developed into different types of blues itself but also influenced different types of music. Delta and Chicago blues both focus on guitar music, and this influence can be seen in modern rock music. Also, the strong expression of feelings has been passed down to rock music. Expressing emotions is a big part of rap music, too. Just like in blues, rap artists openly talk about their experiences and feelings, often sharing social messages, which was a big part of the original Delta blues.
practicePassage.ja: ブルースはブルース自体のさまざまな形態に発展しただけでなく、他のジャンルにも影響を与えた。ロックンロール、ジャズ、ソウルミュージックなど、多くの現代音楽はブルースに根ざしている。ブルースのリズム、コード進行、感情表現は、これらの新しいジャンルの基盤となった。今日でもブルースは世界中で演奏され、アメリカの音楽遺産の最も重要な部分の一つとして認められている。
